description: Using volatile (or no barrier) to coordinate threads, which prevents some compiler optimizations but provides no hardware ordering or atomicity.
body: Failure mode: shared flags/counters are marked volatile and assumed to be thread-safe; volatile stops the compiler caching the value but emits no fence and gives no atomicity, so on weakly-ordered CPUs reads and writes reorder and updates are lost. Detection signals: rare data races flagged by TSan; bug reproduces on ARM but not x86; corruption only under high concurrency. Do not use volatile as a synchronization primitive. Safer fallback: use atomics with explicit acquire/release (or seq_cst) ordering or a proper lock, document the ordering, and verify with a thread sanitizer plus a stress test on the weakest target.
